card.title: cross-viewport UI reliability and layout hardening
card.summary: Unify phone, browser, and installed-app geometry so menu, play, Options, and Pause share measured safe content, status, overlay, motion, and fallback-background rules.
card.objective: Remove physical-device clipping, overlap, inconsistent sibling layouts, uncomfortable trail motion, fake scroll affordances, and purple safe-area bleed without introducing screenshot-specific offsets.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu and play use one contained two-row status layout beside Pause at phone widths.
Options and Pause share guide, control-row, scroll, and pinned-action geometry.
Physical iPhone text remains inside status, guide, row, and button borders.
Page, app, Phaser, theme, and installed-app fallback backgrounds do not expose purple safe-area bands.
Fresh physical iPhone Safari and installed-PWA proof confirms the corrected release.
discoveries: Fresh physical-iPhone production screenshots showed native glyph widths and line boxes exceeding the ideal canvas reserve.
Status rows, guide copy, toggle descriptions, and clipped-edge scroll fragments shared the same optimistic fitting failure.
nextActions: Review the PR #75 build on physical iPhone Safari or the next explicitly approved production release.
Complete only if the phone review confirms no clipping, overlap, border collision, purple bleed, sibling drift, or uncomfortable motion.
blockers: Fresh operator physical-iPhone proof of the corrected build is still required.
evidence: Mazer draft PR #75 is at ee20811b2d65dbdc6f36ba8f0b1684224db06972.
The exact branch passed 49 files / 363 tests, lint, build, and diff checks.
Local 390x844 DPR3 proof reported zero text-fit, overlap, offscreen, guide-containment, button-containment, console, or page errors.
Preview deployment dpl_DszBuTLSr4TaU1QxFMqFJ2t8y1Nm is READY at https://fawxzzy-mazer-bsygbvwk7-fawxzzy.vercel.app.

entry.headline: Physical-iPhone text containment correction is ready for device review
completed: Added shared physical-width reserves and compact padding for native phone text.
Separated toggle descriptions from borders and removed fake clipped-edge card fragments.
Verified the exact PR branch locally and on its hosted preview.
discovered: The prior proof measured ideal canvas text but did not reserve enough width and height for physical iPhone rendering.
next: Collect fresh physical-iPhone screenshots from the corrected build.
Keep the card in Review until that evidence is clean.

card.title: normalize browser UI layout across viewports
card.summary: Normalize responsive browser geometry so narrow layouts cannot overlap, escape, or replay stale dimensions after viewport changes.
card.objective: Recompute layout from the active content rectangle while reserving for physical-device text metrics and preserving only semantic user intent.
acceptanceCriteria: No viewport-derived geometry is persisted across incompatible browser states.
Menu, Options, play, Pause, and dialogs remain contained through supported viewport transitions.
Physical-phone glyph width and line height are represented by the shared fitting contract.
Native maximize, restore, browser-chrome, and phone evidence have deterministic expected outcomes.
discoveries: Physical-iPhone screenshots exposed a text-metric reserve gap even though ideal 390x844 diagnostics passed.
The correction remains fully layout-derived and adds no persisted geometry.
nextActions: Collect fresh physical-iPhone proof for PR #75.
Extend the deterministic fixture to native maximize, restore, and browser-chrome changes.
blockers: Fresh physical-iPhone acceptance and native maximize/restore automation remain open.
evidence: Mazer draft PR #75 is at ee20811b2d65dbdc6f36ba8f0b1684224db06972.
The exact branch passed 49 files / 363 tests, lint, build, and diff checks.
Hosted 390x844 preview diagnostics report zero text-fit, overlap, offscreen, console, or page errors.

entry.headline: Browser layout fitting now reserves for physical iPhone text metrics
completed: Added physical-width safety ratios without persisting viewport geometry.
Verified responsive status, guide, controls, scrolling, and buttons on the hosted phone preview.
discovered: Ideal canvas text measurements were insufficient to guarantee physical-device containment.
next: Run the corrected build on the physical iPhone.
Retain native browser transition automation as the remaining persistence lane.
blockers: Physical-device acceptance and native transition proof remain open.

card.title: shared menu and play run status panel
card.summary: Use one two-row status component in menu AI and active play for time, contextual rank, score out of 100, and maze level.
card.objective: Make menu and play progression information visually identical, readable, and contained while preserving the canonical score-data boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu and play consume the same status-panel layout and formatting contract.
Time, contextual Rank, Score out of 100, and Maze Lvl remain inside their phone and desktop borders.
The play panel fits its measured lane beside Pause without overlap.
Displayed score remains traceable to the canonical run-quality contract.
discoveries: Physical-iPhone production screenshots showed the second status row crossing the right border in menu and play.
The shared fix now reserves physical width in both contexts.
nextActions: Review the corrected panel on a physical iPhone.
Confirm final score-field authority before terminal completion.
blockers: Fresh physical-iPhone proof and final canonical score-field traceability remain required.
evidence: Mazer draft PR #75 is at ee20811b2d65dbdc6f36ba8f0b1684224db06972.
Local 390x844 DPR3 and hosted preview proof report contained 252x60 menu and 248x60 play panels with textFits true.
The exact branch passed 49 files / 363 tests, lint, build, and diff checks.

entry.headline: Shared status panel text containment is ready for device review
completed: Increased the compact panel reserve and reduced compact type pressure through one shared menu/play contract.
Verified both status contexts locally and on the hosted phone preview.
discovered: The physical iPhone rendered the second status row wider than the prior proof model.
blockers: Physical-device acceptance and score authority remain open.
